title: Sample preparation to mass spectrometer using ZipTipC18

steps: Calibrate the P-10 pipette to 10 μL.
Name 4 microtubes of 1.5 mL with the respective reagents: ACN 100% (I) TFA 0.1% (I) TFA 0.1% (II) ACN 100% (II) Add 1 mL of the respective solutions to the tubes above.
Name new 0.2 mL microtubes with sample names followed by “zt”.
Aliquot 50 μL of ACN 100% in each “zt” tube.
During the entire process, no air should enter through ZipTip. Pipetting should be done slowly to maximize chemical interactions
Cleaning and preparation: ACN 100% (I) Pipette the acetonitrile from the ACN 100% (I) tube 10 times, discarding the contents on the kimwipes
Fit the ZipTip with the pipette already attached, so as not to pass air through the "chromatographic column". Hold down until you start the procedure with the first pipetting.
Equilibrate: TFA 0.1% (I) Pipette the acetonitrile from the TFA 0.1% (I) tube 10 times, discarding the contents on the kimwipes
Binding of pepitides (analyte): sample tube Homogenize the sample with ZipTip 20 times
Wash: TFA 0.1% (II) Pipette the acetonitrile from the TFA 0.1% (II) tube 10 times, discarding the contents on the kimwipes
Elution: ACN 100% (II) Homogenize 20 times in the “zt” tube, already with 50 μL of ACN100%
Shutdown: ACN 100% (II) Pipette the acetonitrile from the ACN 100% (II) 10 times, discarding in the “zt” tube.
creator: Neilier Junior
